MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-12-2025 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-22-2024 Continued To Jun-30-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Certain Of Defendants' Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. TAMARAH PREVOST, Esq. of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, LLP (tel: 650-697-6000) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JONATHAN PEARCE, Esq. of Fox Rothschild LLP (tel: 415-364-5540) for Defendants (via Zoom videoconference).

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PLANNED BUILDING SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Certain Of Defendants' Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. Both parties stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn. Plaintiff's counsel signs the stipulation. Defendant's counsel authorizes the clerk to sign the stipulation on his behalf. Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

The motion requests for production of documents is granted. The motion is not untimely because the parties agreed to extend the motion to compel deadline. By December 23, 2024, Plaintiff shall (1) serve amended responses to the requests for production that unequivocally state whether non-privileged documents that are responsive to each request exist and (2) produce all such documents. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Certain Of Defendant'S Request For Admissions, Set One
Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. TAMARAH PREVOST, Esq. of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, LLP (tel: 650-697-6000) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JONATHAN PEARCE, Esq. of Fox Rothschild LLP (tel: 415-364-5540) for Defendants (via Zoom videoconference).

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PLANNED BUILDING SERVICES, INC.' Motion To Compel Responses To Certain Of Defendant'S Request For Admissions, Set One.

Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. Both parties stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn. Plaintiff's counsel signs the stipulation. Defendant's counsel authorizes the clerk to sign the stipulation on his behalf. Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

The motion requests for admission is granted because the admissions sought are discoverable. The motion is not untimely because the parties agreed to extend the motion to compel deadline. Plaintiff shall serve amended responses that unequivocally admit, deny, or state a lack of knowledge sufficient to admit or deny the requests for admission by December 23, 2024. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Certain Of Defendant'S Employment Form Interrogatories, Set One
Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. TAMARAH PREVOST, Esq. of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, LLP (tel: 650-697-6000) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JONATHAN PEARCE, Esq. of Fox Rothschild LLP (tel: 415-364-5540) for Defendants (via Zoom videoconference).

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PLANNED BUILDING SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Certain Of Defendant'S Employment Form Interrogatories, Set One.

Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. Both parties stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn. Plaintiff's counsel signs the stipulation. Defendant's counsel authorizes the clerk to sign the stipulation on his behalf. Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

The motion regarding form interrogatories is granted for the same reasons Defendants' motion regarding requests for admission is granted. The motion is not untimely because the parties agreed to extend the motion to compel deadline. Plaintiff shall serve amended responses that substantively answer the interrogatory by December 23, 2024. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Certain Of Defendant'S Special Interrogatories, Set One
Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. TAMARAH PREVOST, Esq. of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, LLP (tel: 650-697-6000) for Plaintiff.
Atty. JONATHAN PEARCE, Esq. of Fox Rothschild LLP (tel: 415-364-5540) for Defendants (via Zoom videoconference).

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PLANNED BUILDING SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Certain Of Defendant'S Special Interrogatories, Set One.

Plaintiff contests the tentative ruling. Both parties stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn. Plaintiff's counsel signs the stipulation. Defendant's counsel authorizes the clerk to sign the stipulation on his behalf. Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

The motion regarding special interrogatories is granted as to special interrogatories 1-3 and 28-153. The motion is otherwise denied. The motion is not untimely because the parties agreed to extend the motion to compel deadline. The parties sufficiently met and conferred prior to Defendants filing the motion. Plaintiff shall serve amended responses that substantively answer the interrogatories by December 23, 2024. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)
